methods.description: This method involves checking and aligning DNA sequences, combining markers, and estimating genetic variability to understand the molecular diversity of the samples.

steps.description: For each marker, both forward and reverse strands were checked using Chromas and aligned manually in GeneDoc.
steps.searchable_summary: Check and align DNA sequences using Chromas and GeneDoc

steps.description: The two markers were combined in a single concatenated set.
steps.searchable_summary: Concatenate two DNA markers into a single set

steps.description: All insertion/deletion events (indels) that involved poly A/T were eliminated from the analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove poly A/T indels from DNA sequence analysis

steps.description: Contiguous indels of more than one base pair (bp) were treated as one mutational event.
steps.searchable_summary: Treat contiguous indels as single mutational events

steps.description: Descriptive statistics of genetic variability, such as haplotype (h) and nucleotide (π) diversities, were estimated in Arlequin 3.5.

methods.method_name: Population Structure and Spatial Genetic Analysis
methods.description: This method analyzes the genetic variance among and within populations, and explores the spatial genetic structure using AMOVA, Mantel test, and Samova analysis.

steps.description: A two-level AMOVA (i.e., subpopulations vs. the total population) resulted in an estimate of 66% of the total genetic variance found among populations.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform two-level AMOVA to estimate genetic variance

steps.description: The Mantel test indicated a non-significant association between genetic and geographic distances for populations.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct Mantel test for genetic and geographic distance association

steps.description: The Samova analysis suggested that three groups (K = 3) maximize the genetic structure in the among-group level.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Samova analysis to determine genetic structure groups

methods.description: This method models geography through a discrete phylogeographic diffusion model and uses Bayesian stochastic search variable selection (BSSVS) in Beast to classify sampling locations.

steps.description: For the phylogeographic reconstruction, we modeled geography through the discrete phylogeographic diffusion model and used the Bayesian stochastic search variable selection (BSSVS) procedure in Beast.
steps.searchable_summary: Model geography using discrete phylogeographic diffusion and BSSVS in Beast

steps.description: We classified sampling locations in four discrete states: southern coastal plain (Southern), northern coastal plain (Northern)...
steps.searchable_summary: Classify sampling locations into discrete geographic states
